title: Tapping for We Can't Talk Anymore
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the feeling of communication breakdown in a long-term relationship
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the frustration and sadness of a relationship where the same conversations keep failing. Use it when you feel stuck in a pattern with someone and don't know how to shift it.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when a conversation with a partner, family member, or close friend keeps going the same way and you are stuck in the frustration of it.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the communication breakdown that has become its own pattern. Use it when the same conversation keeps failing and you are carrying the weight of not knowing how to change what happens between you.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though we keep having the same conversation and it goes nowhere, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feel more alone in this relationship than I expected to, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I don't know how to change what keeps happening between us, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** We can't seem to talk about this without it becoming something else.
**Side of eye:** I feel more alone here than I expected to feel.
**Under eye:** I keep trying the same thing and getting the same result.
**Under nose:** I feel frustrated and sad.
**Chin:** I don't know how to reach them.
**Collarbone:** I don't know if they can hear me.
**Under arm:** This is not what I thought we were building.
**Top of head:** I feel stuck in this pattern.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** Patterns are not permanent.
**Side of eye:** We have been different before. We can be different again.
**Under eye:** One of us can change the pattern first.
**Under nose:** I can try something I have not tried yet.
**Chin:** I can speak to what I need without attacking what they did.
**Collarbone:** We got here slowly. We can find a way out slowly.
**Under arm:** I am willing to try one thing differently.
**Top of head:** This is hard. It is not hopeless.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times. Then write one thing you could try differently in the next conversation.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
